Fern Por-rrrcer- PRACTICEs C ouiralSSIoN
428 J Street . Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
July 18,2005
Kelly C. Quinn
Law Offices of Mark J. Werksman
801 South Figueroa Street, 11m Floor
Los Angeles, CA 90017
Re
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-05-126a
Dear Ms. Quinn:
This letter is in regard to your follow-up request for advice regarding the
provisions of the Political Reform Act.l
In a letter to you dated June 27 , 2005 , staff formally requested that you
provide the name of the candidate referred to in your letter (as specified in
regulation 18329(b)(2)(A)), as well as state whether the questions in your letter
were hypothetical in nature or related to past conduct. When you failed to
respond to this letter, we closed the letter file number (w-05-126). We informed
you of this in our letter of July 7,2005.
Under the provisions of California Code of Regulations 18329(bX8)(B),
unless you identify the candidate and are writing as his or her authorized
representative, the Fair Political Practices Commission cannot address your
questions or provide analysis. The Commission declines to provide advice on
hypothetical inquiries. (Regulation 1 8 3 29(b)(SXD), enclosed.)
I Government Code sections 8 1 000-910 14. Commission regulations appear at Title 2,
sections 18109-18997 of the California Code of Regulations.
s-+ry


File No. W-05-126a
Page2
If you are able to obtain the requested information (identity of the
candidate) in the future, please feel free to submit a new inquiry. your most
recent letter, received July I l,2oo5, merely restates your question and is not
sufficient to initiate a new advice request.
Should you have any questions, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
Counsel
John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel
Legal Division
JW:rd
